description: baseline: pit --help, sum of per-extension load ms, best of 3
precompile walk all .ts deps + package-manager swap manifest .ts->.js for file entries

asi.hypothesis: precompile incomplete + manifest file entries skipped .js swap. fix both

description: agent-loop: single filter pass + sequential short-circuit + Set in-place mutation

asi.hypothesis: agent-loop micro-opts: drop redundant filter, skip toolMap probe when forceSequential, mutate pendingToolCalls Set in place
asi.note: ext_load wall same as prior; agent-loop work invisible to startup bench but visible in tool batches

asi.hypothesis: overlap jiti core lib transpile with parseArgs/migrations
asi.rollback_reason: no measurable wall improvement (4580 vs 4559), within noise. confirms existing comment that prewarm cost equals first .ts extension cost on single-threaded jiti
asi.next_action_hint: leave prewarm exported for SDK callers, do not auto-trigger

name: PiTuned reasoning/call execution speed (prompt prefix size + config)

description: runner: emitContext skip clone+early-out + cache getAllRegisteredTools/getFlags/getRegisteredCommands/getCommand (Map lookup O(1))

asi.hypothesis: unconditional structuredClone every turn (transformContext path) + per-call getter recomputation in TUI render loops. fix: hasHandlers context early-out, drop clone (handler contract: no in-place mutation), memoize getters invalidated by wrapped refreshTools
wins: getCommand O(N) find -> O(1) Map lookup
asi.tests: 65/65 coding-agent runner+discovery+suite, 155/167 agent harness (12 pre-existing windows fails)

description: runner: split before_provider_request (side-effect parallel / mutating serial via pi.markSideEffect) + PI_TIMING instrumentation emit_context_ms + emit_bpr_ms

asi.hypothesis: 21 extensions; only pi-claude-oauth-adapter mutates payload (grep confirmed). split lets side-effect hooks (logs, telemetry, OAuth-watch) run in Promise.all instead of serial. instrument both emitContext and emitBeforeProviderRequest with PI_TIMING=1 logging so per-turn ground truth is measurable
asi.api_change: pi.markSideEffect(handler) — opt-in tagging; default mutating preserves prior semantics
asi.startup_delta: within noise vs run 5 (2645 -> 2744); split only fires per-turn so startup bench cannot observe gain
asi.next: run pit -p with PI_TIMING=1 on real turn, capture METRIC emit_bpr_ms before/after extensions opt into markSideEffect

description: runner: emit() parallel for side-effect events (turn_start/turn_end/session_start/agent_start/etc) + PI_SIDE_EFFECT_EXTENSIONS env-driven before_provider_request tagging in loader

asi.hypothesis: installed extension survey: 98 session_start, 41 session_shutdown, 39 before_agent_start, 27 agent_end, 21 tool_call, 17 turn_start/turn_end, 9 tool_result, 8 context, 5 before_provider_request, 5 after_provider_response — only session_before_* needs serial (cancel short-circuit). Rest are side-effect; parallelize emit() to settle all handlers in one event-loop tick

invariants.emit_error_isolation: one handler throw does not abort siblings

asi.tests: 32/32 runner (3 new: bpr split, env-marking, emit parallel + session_before serial)
asi.env_var: PI_SIDE_EFFECT_EXTENSIONS=<substr1>,<substr2> tags 3rd-party before_provider_request handlers as parallel without patching extension source
asi.bench_delta: wall 4559(run1) -> 4271 (-6%); ext_load 2889->2713 (-6%); per-turn gain only visible with PI_TIMING=1 on real pit -p

description: simplify: HANDLER_SIDE_EFFECT_TAG const (kill magic string x3), emit() hasHandlers gate, cache bpr partition, getCommand calls getRegisteredCommands, dedup bpr try/catch via runHandler, strip narrative comments

fixed: emit() 0-handler hot path: hasHandlers gate before partition alloc
bpr partition cached in _cachedBprPartition (re-alloc -> O(1) re-read per turn)
getCommand bypassed getRegisteredCommands -> commandDiagnostics stale, fixed
stripped: '(CHANGED from upstream)', '5-20ms on 25k-token sessions', '@capyup/pi-goal inspected', '21 extensions in ~/.pi/agent/npm', 'Default-mutating preserves existing semantics'
skipped: Symbol vs string property (style call, const is enough)
asi.bench: wall 4307 within noise of run 7 (4271); no regression

description: PI_TIMING=1 real turn measurement (pit -p ok) — first ground-truth of per-turn instrumentation

asi.hypothesis: baseline structuredClone in emitContext cost 5-20ms; serial emit() of session_start with 15 handlers cost ~45-60ms
asi.interpretation: emit_context_ms 0.2ms confirms structuredClone removal (was 5-20ms). session_start 42ms for 15 handlers = ~2.8ms avg — installed handlers are light; parallelization saves ms not hundreds. Real gain landed but bounded by handler weight
asi.absent: emit_bpr_ms not observed (provider call failed before fire — auth or model resolution); pi-claude-oauth-adapter is sole mutating bpr handler so future runs will show it serial single-handler
asi.next: add emit_*_ms to bench-startup synthetic harness with N=10 synthetic 50ms-sleep handlers to demonstrate parallelization win deterministically

name: PiTuned harness quality: tool-call latency under load

description: baseline: bench-tool-calls-real after harness bringup. Validates agent-loop micro-opts (parallel, delta coalesce, Promise.all listeners) still work.

asi.hypothesis: post-bringup tool-call latency baseline. Confirms previously shipped micro-opts intact: parallel/sequential ratio 2.6-2.7x, hook parallel 10x faster, multi-listener ~ single-listener (Promise.all). Streaming coalesce 16ms cap works (188ms vs raw chunk count).
